# Transformers & Attention

## Attention mechanics
- Core question: "for this token, which other tokens should I attend to?"
- `Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QKŠĶÄ/‚ąöd_k) V` ‚ÄĒ **scaled dot-product attention**
- Per token: **Query** q (what I'm looking for), **Key** k (what I offer for matching), **Value** v (the info I contribute)
- Step 1 embedding + positional: `x_pos = x + PE`; then `qŠĶĘ=xŠĶĘW_q, kŠĶĘ=xŠĶĘW_k, vŠĶĘ=xŠĶĘW_v`
- `QKŠĶÄ` = query-key similarity; `/‚ąöd_k` scales so scores don't get too big (keeps softmax stable)
- softmax ‚Üí attention weights (probabilities); multiply V ‚Üí weighted sum of values
- **Shapes**: `Q,K,V ‚ąą ‚ĄĚ^{n√ód}`; `QKŠĶÄ ‚ąą ‚ĄĚ^{n√ón}`; output `‚ĄĚ^{n√ód}`

## Self vs cross attention
- **Self-attention**: Q,K,V from the **same** sequence. Encoder = unmasked/bidirectional; Decoder = **masked** (causal, look-ahead mask sets future scores to ‚ąí‚ąě ‚Üí 0 after softmax)
- **Cross-attention**: Q from decoder hidden states, K,V from encoder output (one sequence attends to another)
- **Positional encoding**: `PE(pos,2i)=sin(pos/10000^{2i/d})`, `PE(pos,2i+1)=cos(...)`. Absolute vs relative; **RoPE** = rotary, better extrapolation / longer context
- **Multi-head attention**: each head learns a different relationship; `head_i=Attention(Q_i,K_i,V_i)`; concat heads; `d_k = d_model/num_heads`

## Encoder / Decoder layer
- Encoder layer: `x ‚Üí multi-head self-attn ‚Üí add residual ‚Üí LayerNorm ‚Üí FFN ‚Üí add residual ‚Üí LayerNorm`
- FFN `FFN(X)=G(XW‚āĀ+b‚āĀ)W‚āā+b‚āā`, G=ReLU/GeLU; larger hidden = more room to learn
- Decoder layer: masked multi-head self-attn ‚Üí Add&Norm ‚Üí cross-attn ‚Üí Add&Norm ‚Üí FFN ‚Üí Add&Norm

## Interview Q&A
??? question "Why divide by ‚ąöd_k in attention?"
    Dot products of d_k-dim vectors grow with d_k, pushing softmax into saturated regions with tiny gradients. Dividing by ‚ąöd_k keeps the variance ~1 so softmax stays well-conditioned.

??? question "Why multi-head instead of one big attention?"
    Each head can attend to a different relationship/subspace (e.g. syntax vs co-reference) at lower per-head dimension; concatenating them gives a richer representation than a single head of the same total size.

??? question "What does the causal mask do in the decoder?"
    It sets attention scores for future positions to ‚ąí‚ąě (‚Üí 0 after softmax) so each token can only attend to itself and earlier tokens, preserving autoregressive generation.
